site.title: Diabetes Awareness
site.footer_tagline: Empowering people living with diabetes with knowledge, community, and hope — one step at a time.
site.footer_note: Made with care for the diabetes community.

welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.subtitle: Millions of people around the world live with diabetes. We're here to help you recognise the signs, understand your treatment options, and connect with a community that truly gets it.

paragraphs: A few years ago, my dad walked into a hospital for a routine visit and walked out with a life-changing diagnosis - Severe Type 2 diabetes. The damage was already done. He was told to start insulin immediately, and most likely, he'd be on it for the result of his life.

No dramatic warning signs. No moment where he thought, 'something is really wrong.' It crept up quietly, the way diabetes so often does, and by the time it showed up, it had already been building for years.

labels.elderly_callout: Up to 52.1% adults over 65 have prediabetes.

hero.headline: Understanding diabetes. Together.
hero.subtitle: It is easier to fight diabetes when you understand it. At the end of the day, it is not just a matter of insulin. But also the lifestyle and genetics.

sections.title: If you have any of these symptoms, visit a doctor.
sections.body: Diabetes can develop gradually and its early warning signs are often easy to dismiss. Recognising them early gives you the best chance of managing the condition effectively and avoiding serious complications.
bullet_items: Tingling or numbness in hands and feet

sections.title: Do you want to know more about your medication?
sections.body: Managing diabetes often involves a combination of lifestyle changes and medication. Understanding what each treatment does — and how to use it safely — puts you firmly in control of your own health journey.
bullet_items: Insulin therapy: types, dosing, and delivery methods
Oral medications and how they work
Diet and exercise as first-line treatment
When and how to adjust your medication plan

sections.title: Looking for a diabetes community?
sections.body: You are not alone. Hundreds of thousands of people living with diabetes have found strength, advice, and friendship through shared experience. Our community is open to everyone — newly diagnosed or a long-time warrior.
sections.image_alt: Diabetes community members showing CGM patches
bullet_items: Connect with others who truly understand
Share your story and inspire someone else
Access peer-reviewed tips on daily management
Join local and online support groups
